issues.description: The source packet contains no provider candidate or unknown output field, so the expected provider_schema_invalid rejection depends on an invisible schema defect that cannot be verified from the fixture.
issues.remediation_requirement: Include the candidate output containing the unknown top-level field in the source fixture, or remove the unsupported rejection oracle.

issues.description: The expected risk material strengthens higher fixed cost into a recurrent-cost mechanism and external dependency into an operational interruption with unknown contractual resilience, none of which is explicit in the source.
issues.remediation_requirement: Keep mechanisms at higher fixed cost, external dependency, and unconfirmed demand, or add facts supporting recurrence, interruption, and contractual resilience.

issues.description: Cluster 001 is materially coherent, but Spanish adds a feminine referent while Russian uses a masculine-form referent and EN/ZH remain neutral; the decision semantics are preserved with a bounded localization note.
issues.remediation_requirement: Align referent gender across locale variants or document a deliberate locale-neutral translation convention.

issues.description: The trial-city source gives a known trial budget, an uncertain remote-work assumption, and a missing return-housing option, but it never states that two homes will be paid for simultaneously; the expected double-housing-cost risk invents that commitment.
issues.remediation_requirement: Replace the double-housing-cost risk with a conditional housing-continuity or return-option risk, or add an explicit overlapping-housing commitment.

issues.description: Known lease terms, an uncertain growth forecast, and a missing exit clause create a material gap but no explicit incompatible claim pair to reconcile or preserve.
issues.remediation_requirement: Add two explicit incompatible capacity, lease, or forecast claims, or classify the fixture as critically incomplete and retain only the exit-clause clarification.

issues.description: Known weekly care needs, unequal availability, an availability assumption, and missing backup responsibility do not form two explicit incompatible claims, so contradiction reconciliation is unsupported.
issues.remediation_requirement: Add explicit conflicting availability or responsibility claims, or reclassify the fixture as critically incomplete and ask only about backup responsibility.
